page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-32 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, aligning with compute-heavy workload profiles.

This instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the dedicated CPU allocation and the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4a-highcpu-32 is engineered for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads such as batch processing, distributed compute tasks, web serving, and continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines where raw processing power is prioritized over memory capacity.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the n4a family, configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. This allocation provides a compute-focused resource density of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance features no local storage and no integrated GPU accelerators, requiring external block storage for data persistence. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for high-compute efficiency, this instance is optimized for processing-intensive workloads that do not demand large memory footprints. Appropriate use cases include batch processing, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, web serving, and distributed compute tasks where CPU performance is the primary bottleneck.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and a lean memory profile for processing-heavy workloads.
